text: To set effective project goals, use the SMART framework. This means goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of "finish the report," say "complete the first draft by Friday." Next, break your project into milestones—these are key checkpoints to track progress, like finishing research before design. Each milestone should produce a deliverable, such as a document or prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment: confirm that everyone involved, from your team to clients, agrees on the goals. This prevents confusion later and keeps the project on track from start to finish.
